title: Evangelion is the best selling anime DVD of 2010 Japan
display_title: Evangelion is the best selling anime DVD of 2010 Japan
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion is the best-selling anime DVD of 2010 (Japan)             Quote Post by visionfactory » Mon Dec 20, 2010 10:03 pm Japan's Yearly DVD Ranking by Oricon: 1. “Michael Jackson THIS IS IT Deluxe • Collector’s • Edition” – Michael Jackson : (802,656) 2. “ARASHI Anniversary Tour 5×10″ – Arashi : (786,303) 3. “Evangelion Shin Gekijoban: EVANGELION: 2.22 YOU CAN (NOT) ADVANCE.” – animation : (373,820) 4. “Michael Jackson THIS IS IT Collector’s • Edition” – Michael Jackson : (322,373) 5. “EXILE LIVE TOUR 2010 FANTASY” – EXILE : (315,053) 6. “AVATAR” – movie (Sam Worthington) : (306,901) 7. “5×10 ALL the BEST! CLIPS 1999-2009″ – Arashi : (228,444) 8. “KAT-TUN LIVE Break the Records” – KAT-TUN : (198,672) 9. “2012 Standard Edition” – movie (John Cusack) : (193,698) 10. “Alice in Wonderland” – movie (Johnny Depp) : (185,298)

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Dec 20, 2010 10:27 pm And not a single other anime in the top 10. What do the Blu-ray rankings look like? It's worth noting that Michael Jackson and whoever Arashi is each sold over one million combined DVDs in the top ten. I doubt that any concert DVD in the US sells like that. Do the Japanese have no taste for film?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 20, 2010 10:41 pm The next highest was the One Piece movie (some special edition version) coming in at #12, Up at #16, Summer Wars at #24. Something interesting is that My Neighbor Totoro was ranked #42, released in 2001, but in 2010 it sold 79,384 copies.

[]: Japan sure loves them some Michael, I reckon.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Dec 20, 2010 11:42 pm Ornette wrote: Something interesting is that My Neighbor Totoro was ranked #42, released in 2001, but in 2010 it sold 79,384 copies.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Mon Dec 20, 2010 11:45 pm drinian wrote: What do the Blu-ray rankings look like?

[]: *claps dust off hands* Another puzzle solved.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Mon Dec 20, 2010 11:47 pm visionfactory wrote: Sorry I don't think there is a separately chart for Blu-ray releases.

[]: Re: Japan sure loves them some Michael, I reckon.             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Tue Dec 21, 2010 12:02 am Killer Bee wrote: It was just released on Blu-rei, wasn't it? Maybe that has something to do with it.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Dec 21, 2010 1:02 am drinian wrote: And not a single other anime in the top 10. What do the Blu-ray rankings look like?

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Dec 21, 2010 1:05 am drinian wrote: It's worth noting that Michael Jackson and whoever Arashi is each sold over one million combined DVDs in the top ten. I doubt that any concert DVD in the US sells like that. Do the Japanese have no taste for film?

[]: Re: Japan sure loves them some Michael, I reckon.             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Tue Dec 21, 2010 3:30 am Killer Bee wrote: It was just released on Blu-rei, wasn't it? Maybe that has something to do with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:27 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Last time we had news, 2.22 was the #1 blu-ray in Japanese history (357,000) beating out Michael Jackson (351,000) http://www.oricon.co.jp/news/rankmusic/76798/full/

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 21, 2010 9:56 am This means more Money for 3.0. Meh be Happy. There's pretty, pretty Kaworu footage on the way HEHEHE FINALLY. Evangelion ought to get the respect it deserves, it's up there with "Faust."

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue Dec 21, 2010 1:43 pm Kendrix wrote: This means more Money for 3.0. Meh be Happy. There's pretty, pretty Kaworu footage on the way HEHEHE FINALLY. Evangelion ought to get the respect it deserves, it's up there with "Faust."

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:39 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Sales do not equate with respect most of the times, it just equates with shitton of money; 'just saying. Also, not really surprised with Eva, it certainly (can) advance on sales. Hur.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Tue Dec 21, 2010 6:43 pm Kendrix wrote: it's not an obscure nerd-thing anymore

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Dec 22, 2010 9:40 pm Here's the BD rankings: http://www.oricon.co.jp/news/rankmusic/83258/full/ No surprise, 2.22 is #1, followed by Michael Jackson.
